name: playwright-testing
description: Automatically activated when user works with Playwright tests, mentions Playwright configuration, asks about selectors/locators/page objects, or has files matching *.spec.ts in e2e or tests directories. Provides Playwright-specific expertise for E2E and integration testing.
capabilities: Create Playwright test configurations with projects, browsers, and reporters
Write resilient tests using role-based locators and chaining patterns
Implement the Page Object Model with reusable, maintainable page classes
Mock API responses and test error scenarios with network interception
Debug tests using traces, screenshots, and the Playwright Inspector
Set up authentication with storage state and custom test fixtures
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: This is a legitimate Playwright testing skill providing E2E testing guidance. The 209 static findings are almost entirely false positives triggered by documentation patterns (code blocks in markdown) rather than executable code. The setup script validates local Playwright installation using standard dev tooling patterns. No credential theft, data exfiltration, or malicious patterns detected.
valueStatement: Writing reliable end-to-end tests is challenging without proper guidance. This skill provides expert-level Playwright knowledge including locators, page objects, fixtures, and debugging techniques to help you build robust browser automation tests.
limitations: Does not execute tests directly - provides guidance for test creation
Does not install Playwright browsers or dependencies automatically
Requires existing Playwright project structure for best results
Cannot modify existing test files - only provides recommendations

useCases.title: Build E2E test suites
useCases.description: Create comprehensive end-to-end test suites using Playwright best practices for selectors, assertions, and test organization.

useCases.title: Test web applications
useCases.description: Write reliable browser automation tests for web applications including form testing, API mocking, and visual regression checks.

useCases.title: Scale test coverage
useCases.description: Set up cross-browser testing, parallel execution, and CI/CD integration for production-quality test pipelines.

promptTemplates.title: Get started with Playwright
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write my first Playwright test. I want to test a login form with email and password fields and a submit button.

promptTemplates.title: Improve test selectors
promptTemplates.prompt: My Playwright tests are failing because elements cannot be found. Show me how to use role-based locators instead of CSS selectors for better reliability.

promptTemplates.title: Create page objects
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to refactor my Playwright tests to use the Page Object Model. I have tests for login, dashboard, and user profile pages.

promptTemplates.title: Mock API responses
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I mock API responses in Playwright to test error handling, slow networks, and different data scenarios without a real backend?

bestPractices: Use role-based locators (getByRole, getByLabel) instead of CSS selectors for accessible, resilient tests
Prefer auto-waiting assertions over explicit waits to avoid flaky tests
Organize tests with Page Objects to separate page logic from test assertions
antiPatterns: Using CSS selectors with nth-child or index-based positioning - breaks when UI changes
Using waitForLoadState('networkidle') - unreliable with WebSockets and analytics
Writing tests without proper assertions - always verify expected outcomes
Hardcoding credentials in tests - use environment variables and storage state instead

faq.question: What Playwright versions are supported?
faq.answer: This skill covers Playwright 1.0+ features. Role-based locators require 1.27+. Run npx playwright --version to check.

faq.question: How many tests can I run in parallel?
faq.answer: Set workers in playwright.config.ts. Use fullyParallel: true for maximum speed. Consider browser limitations.

faq.question: Can I use this with other testing frameworks?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on @playwright/test. For Jest or Vitest, use Playwright as a library rather than the test runner.

faq.question: Is my test data safe?
faq.answer: The skill only reads and provides guidance. It does not store or transmit test data. Use storageState for authentication.

faq.question: Why are my tests flaky?
faq.answer: Common causes: using networkidle, missing assertions, dynamic content not awaited, or element state changes.

faq.question: How does this compare to Selenium?
faq.answer: Playwright offers auto-waiting, built-in assertions, better locators, and faster execution. No external driver needed.
